categories.discovery: Find, locate, observe, or detect without retrieving content.
categories.retrieval: Obtain, load, or pull content into the agent's working context.
categories.analysis: Reason over, compute on, score, or judge content.
categories.transaction: Commit value, exchange, or external state-changing actions.
categories.modification: Change, restructure, or remove existing state.
categories.creation: Bring new entities or artifacts into existence.
categories.notification: Signal, communicate, or publish to recipients.
categories.mechanics: Protocol primitives for agent coordination and lifecycle.

QUERY.description: Structured request for matching records or state.

DISCOVER.description: Surface available resources, agents, or capabilities.

SUMMARIZE.description: Produce a condensed account of input.

PLAN.description: Produce a sequence of intended actions.

CONFIRM.description: Affirm a stated fact or intent.

PROPOSE.description: Offer a tentative action for review.

NOTIFY.description: Inform a recipient of an event or fact.

BROWSE.description: Traverse a hierarchy or listing without a target.

DETECT.description: Recognize the presence of a signal or condition.

ENUMERATE.description: List every member of a defined set.

ENVISION.description: Project a hypothetical state for inspection.

EXAMINE.description: Investigate the details of an item.

EXPLORE.description: Open-ended traversal to map an unknown space.

EXPOSE.description: Make an internal state available externally.

FIND.description: Locate an item matching given criteria.

FOLLOW.description: Move along a path or feed of items.

IDENTIFY.description: Determine the identity or class of an entity.

INDEX.description: Build a lookup structure over a corpus.

INSPECT.description: Closely examine an item's structure or contents.

INTROSPECT.description: Examine internal state of self or another agent.

LIST.description: Return the members of a collection.

LISTEN.description: Wait for inbound events on a channel.

LOCATE.description: Determine the position or address of a known entity.

LOOKUP.description: Resolve a key against a known mapping.

MATCH.description: Test whether items satisfy a pattern.

MONITOR.description: Continuously observe for changes or thresholds.

OBSERVE.description: Passively watch state without acting on it.

PINPOINT.description: Narrow a location or cause to a precise point.

POLL.description: Repeatedly query for state at intervals.

PROBE.description: Send a controlled stimulus to elicit a response.

RECOGNIZE.description: Identify a known pattern within input.

RESOLVE.description: Translate a reference to a concrete entity.

REVEAL.description: Make a previously hidden value visible.

SAMPLE.description: Take a representative subset for inspection.

SCAN.description: Systematically inspect a range of items.

SEARCH.description: Scan a corpus or index for matches.

SENSE.description: Acquire a low-level signal from the environment.

SPOT.description: Notice an item of interest among many.

SURVEY.description: Sample broadly to characterize a population.

TRACE.description: Follow a chain of causes or references.

TRACK.description: Follow an entity's state across time.

UNCOVER.description: Reveal something previously hidden or missed.

WATCH.description: Observe a target for change events.

ACCESS.description: Obtain authorized use of a resource.

ACQUIRE.description: Take ownership or possession of an asset.

ASSEMBLE.description: Combine fetched parts into a whole.

BUFFER.description: Hold incoming data temporarily before use.

CACHE.description: Store a copy for faster subsequent access.

CAPTURE.description: Record a transient signal to durable form.

CHECKIN.description: Return a checked-out item with updates.

CHECKOUT.description: Acquire an item from shared storage for use.

COLLECT.description: Aggregate items into a single set.

COMPILE.description: Build a composite from constituent inputs.

DOWNLOAD.description: Copy a remote artifact to local storage.

DUMP.description: Emit a complete byte-level copy of state.

EXPORT.description: Render and emit data for external use.

EXTRACT.description: Pull a substructure out of a larger artifact.

FETCH.description: Retrieve a resource by identifier or location.

IMPORT.description: Bring external data into the system.

INGEST.description: Accept and prepare external input for use.

LOAD.description: Bring data into memory or runtime state.

MIRROR.description: Maintain a synchronized copy of a source.

OBTAIN.description: Acquire possession or access to an item.

OPEN.description: Establish a handle or session over a resource.

PREFETCH.description: Retrieve in advance of expected use.

PULL.description: Initiate transfer from a remote source.

RECEIVE.description: Accept inbound delivery of an item.

REPLICATE.description: Produce a duplicate of an existing artifact.

RETRIEVE.description: Recover a stored item into working context.

SNAPSHOT.description: Capture a point-in-time copy of state.

STREAM.description: Deliver content in a continuous flow.

SUBSCRIBE.description: Open a continuous read against a feed.

AGGREGATE.description: Combine multiple values into a summary.

ANALYZE.description: Break input into components and assess them.

ANNOTATE.description: Attach commentary or metadata to an item.

ASSESS.description: Form a judgment about quality or risk.

ATTRIBUTE.description: Assign provenance or causation to an item.

CALCULATE.description: Apply arithmetic to derive a value.

CALIBRATE.description: Adjust a model to match a reference.

CATEGORIZE.description: Place an item into a taxonomic group.

CLASSIFY.description: Assign an item to a defined class.

CLUSTER.description: Group similar items by inherent structure.

COUNT.description: Determine the cardinality of a set.

DECODE.description: Recover content from an encoded form.

DECONSTRUCT.description: Break an artifact into its components.

DEDUCE.description: Reach a conclusion by formal reasoning.

DIAGNOSE.description: Identify the cause of an observed condition.

EVALUATE.description: Compute the value of an expression or condition.

EXTRAPOLATE.description: Project values beyond the known range.

FORECAST.description: Project values across a time horizon.

GENERALIZE.description: Extend a pattern beyond observed cases.

GRADE.description: Assign a level of quality or correctness.

GROUP.description: Partition items by a chosen attribute.

HASH.description: Reduce input to a fixed-size digest.

JUDGE.description: Render a decision based on criteria.

MEASURE.description: Quantify a property against a unit.

MODEL.description: Construct a representation of a system.

NORMALIZE.description: Bring values to a standard form.

PARSE.description: Convert serialized input into structured form.

PREDICT.description: Forecast a future value or event.

PRIORITIZE.description: Order items by importance or urgency.

PROFILE.description: Characterize the behavior of an artifact.

PROJECT.description: Extend a trend forward in time.

QUANTIFY.description: Express a property as a number.

REDUCE.description: Fold a sequence into a single result.

SCORE.description: Assign a numeric judgment to an item.

SIMULATE.description: Run a model to produce hypothetical outcomes.

SORT.description: Reorder items by a defined key.

STANDARDIZE.description: Apply a uniform format across items.

SYNTHESIZE.description: Combine sources into a new coherent whole.

TRANSFORM.description: Convert input from one form to another.

ASSIGN.description: Designate a resource to a target.

BARGAIN.description: Discuss to reach mutually acceptable terms.

BID.description: Submit a price in an auction.

BILL.description: Charge a party for goods or services.

BORROW.description: Take an asset under obligation to return.

BUY.description: Exchange value for goods or services.

CHARGE.description: Apply a debit against an account.

CLAIM.description: Assert a right to compensation or benefit.

CLEAR.description: Confirm and complete a pending exchange.

CREDIT.description: Apply a positive entry to an account.

DEBIT.description: Apply a negative entry to an account.

DISBURSE.description: Pay out funds from a holding.

HIRE.description: Engage a party for paid work.

LEND.description: Provide an asset under expected return.

ORDER.description: Submit a request for goods or services.

PLEDGE.description: Bind value as collateral or commitment.

PROCESS.description: Apply standard handling to an item.

PURCHASE.description: Acquire an item by paying for it.

QUOTE.description: Provide a price for goods or services.

REIMBURSE.description: Compensate a party for an expense.

REQUEST.description: Ask another party to perform an action.

RESERVE.description: Hold a resource against future use.

RETIRE.description: Permanently remove an instrument from use.

SELL.description: Transfer goods or services for value.

SUBMIT.description: Send work or input for processing.

TRANSFER.description: Move value or ownership between parties.

UNDERWRITE.description: Assume risk for a financial instrument.

ALTER.description: Change a property of an entity.

AMEND.description: Add corrections to an existing record.

APPEND.description: Add content to the end of an item.

BRANCH.description: Create a parallel line of changes.

DECOMPRESS.description: Restore content to its uncompressed form.

DOWNGRADE.description: Move to a lower version or tier.

DUPLICATE.description: Produce a copy of an existing item.

ENCRYPT.description: Render content unreadable without a key.

EXTEND.description: Lengthen the duration or scope of an item.

FORK.description: Create a divergent copy for separate evolution.

INSERT.description: Place content at a specified position.

MIGRATE.description: Move data or workload to a new substrate.

MODIFY.description: Change one or more attributes of an item.

OVERRIDE.description: Supersede a default with a specific value.

PREPEND.description: Add content to the beginning of an item.

REASSIGN.description: Change the designation of a resource.

REBASE.description: Replay changes onto a new base state.

RECONFIGURE.description: Change configuration of a running system.

REDACT.description: Obscure sensitive content while retaining structure.

REFRESH.description: Reload to incorporate the latest state.

REMOVE.description: Eliminate an item from a collection.

RENAME.description: Assign a new identifier to an existing item.

RENEW.description: Extend the validity of an instrument.

REORGANIZE.description: Restructure the layout of a collection.

REPATH.description: Change the location reference of an item.

REROUTE.description: Send traffic via a new path.

RESCHEDULE.description: Move a planned event to a new time.

RESET.description: Restore to a defined initial state.

RESIZE.description: Change the size of a resource.

RESTORE.description: Recover a prior state from backup.

RESTRUCTURE.description: Change the shape or schema of an item.

REVERT.description: Undo recent changes to a known state.

REVISE.description: Produce an improved version of content.

ROLLBACK.description: Revert to a prior committed state.

SET.description: Assign a value to a named slot.

SPLIT.description: Divide an item into multiple parts.

SWAP.description: Exchange the positions of two items.

TRUNCATE.description: Cut an item to a shorter length.

UNARCHIVE.description: Restore an archived item to active use.

UPDATE.description: Replace an existing value with a new one.

UPGRADE.description: Move to a higher version or tier.

COMPOSE.description: Build a structured artifact from parts.

CREATE.description: Bring a new entity into existence.

DECLARE.description: State the existence or value of an item.

DEFINE.description: Establish the meaning or shape of a term.

DESIGN.description: Specify the form and behavior of an artifact.

ESTABLISH.description: Found or set up an entity or rule.

FORGE.description: Form an artifact through deliberate work.

FORMULATE.description: Express precisely as a definition or plan.

GENERATE.description: Produce output from input or parameters.

INSTANTIATE.description: Create an instance from a definition.

MINT.description: Produce a new instance of an instrument.

ORIGINATE.description: Bring something into being for the first time.

PRODUCE.description: Bring forth an output from a process.

PROVISION.description: Bring a new resource into existence and ready it.

RENDER.description: Produce a presentation form from a model.

SCAFFOLD.description: Generate baseline structure for an artifact.

SEED.description: Populate initial state for a new entity.

SPAWN.description: Initiate a new process or agent.

BROADCAST.description: Send a message to all recipients.

COMMENT.description: Attach a remark to an item.

ECHO.description: Return a copy of received input.

FORWARD.description: Relay a message to another recipient.

INFORM.description: Convey a fact to a recipient.

RELAY.description: Pass a signal through an intermediary.

REMIND.description: Prompt about a previously known item.

REPLY.description: Send a return message in a thread.

REPORT.description: Provide a structured account of state.

SIGNAL.description: Convey a discrete state to listeners.

SUMMON.description: Call a party to attend or respond.

ATTEST.description: Provide signed evidence of a fact.

AUDITLOG.description: Record an action in an immutable log.

AUTHENTICATE.description: Establish the identity of a party.

AWAIT.description: Pause until a condition or event.

CHECKPOINT.description: Record state for possible later resumption.

DISPATCH.description: Route a request to a handler.

FALLBACK.description: Switch to an alternative when primary fails.

GOVERN.description: Apply policy to constrain an action.

HANDOFF.description: Transfer ownership of a task to another agent.

INVOKE.description: Call a named capability or method.

QUARANTINE.description: Isolate a suspect artifact pending review.

ROUTE.description: Choose a path for a request or message.

YIELD.description: Voluntarily release control to another party.

ACTIVATE.description: Bring an entity into an operational state.

ATTACH.description: Connect a peripheral or accessory to a host.

BIND.description: Associate a name with an endpoint.

BOOTSTRAP.description: Initialize a system from minimal inputs.

CANCEL.description: Withdraw a pending or future action.

CORDON.description: Block new work on a node without stopping it.

CUTOVER.description: Switch traffic from old system to new.

DEACTIVATE.description: Take an entity out of operational state.

DELIVER.description: Transfer an item to a recipient.

DEMOTE.description: Move an artifact to a lower environment.

DEPLOY.description: Place a build into a runtime environment.

DEPROVISION.description: Tear down and release a provisioned resource.

DETACH.description: Disconnect a peripheral from a host.

DISABLE.description: Turn off a feature or capability.

DRAIN.description: Bleed traffic off a node before stop.

ENABLE.description: Turn on a feature or capability.

ENROLL.description: Register a party in a program.

EVICT.description: Forcibly remove a workload from a host.

EXPIRE.description: Mark an item as past validity.

FAILOVER.description: Switch to a standby on primary failure.

MOUNT.description: Attach a storage volume to a runtime.

OFFBOARD.description: Remove a party from operational status.

ONBOARD.description: Bring a party into operational readiness.

PAIR.description: Form a trusted association between devices.

PROMOTE.description: Advance an artifact to a higher environment.

REBOOK.description: Replace an existing booking with a new one.

RESTART.description: Stop and immediately start a service.

SCHEDULE.description: Place an item on a future timeline.

SHUTDOWN.description: Bring a system to an off state in order.

UNBIND.description: Dissociate a name from an endpoint.

UNCORDON.description: Restore acceptance of new work on a node.

UNDEPLOY.description: Remove a build from a runtime environment.
